---
title: "From microscopic relations to the permutation theorem"
---

This appendix assembles the full derivation chain for the permutation
theorem (@thm-master) and its aligned-representation image, starting from
component-wise relations on one-particle states and ending with the closure
identity. The system is $\Xi_b^-(0)\to K^-(1)\,p(2)\,K^-(3)$ — spin
structure $\tfrac12\to 0+\tfrac12+0$ — which keeps every phase visible while
containing all the mechanisms. The derivation below first uses the toy
standard configuration with the proton along $+\hat z$; the final subsection
maps it to the published proton-along-$-\hat z$ convention of
@LHCb:2021XiBpKK. Every derived relation is verified to machine precision by
`cases/02-xib2pKK/toy_alignment_check.jl`; the map between relations and
tests is tabulated in @sec-app-testmap.

Conventions used throughout (registry rows of the
[conventions chapter](01-conventions.qmd)):
$$
D^{j}_{m'm}(\alpha,\beta,\gamma)=e^{-im'\alpha}d^{j}_{m'm}(\beta)e^{-im\gamma},
\qquad
U(R)\,|j,m\rangle=\sum_{m'}D^{j}_{m'm}(R)\,|j,m'\rangle,
$$
$$
|p,\lambda\rangle
= U\!\big[R_h(\hat p)\big]\,U\!\big[L_z(|\vec p\,|)\big]\,|s,\lambda\rangle,
\qquad
R_h(\hat p) = R_z(\phi)R_y(\theta).
$$

## Microscopic inputs {#sec-app-inputs}

Four independent ingredients; everything else in this appendix is derived.

**(M1) Statistics.** Identical species are excitations of one field;
creation operators satisfy
$a^\dagger_K(p)\,a^\dagger_K(p') = +\,a^\dagger_K(p')\,a^\dagger_K(p)$
(bosons; $-$ for fermions). The multiparticle state used to define the
fixed-frame amplitude,
$$
|f(x;\lambda_2)\rangle
= a^\dagger_K(p_1)\,a^\dagger_p(p_2,\lambda_2)\,a^\dagger_K(p_3)\,|0\rangle,
$$
is the same physical state when the two kaon arguments are interchanged. The
theorem follows by reading this commutation relation through
$M_{\lambda_0\lambda_2}(x)=\langle f(x;\lambda_2)|\,T\,|0,\lambda_0\rangle$:
$$
M_{\lambda_0\lambda_2}(p_3,p_2,p_1) = +\,M_{\lambda_0\lambda_2}(p_1,p_2,p_3).
$$ {#eq-app-theorem}
No frame alignment is involved at this level. The rest of the appendix shows
how @eq-app-theorem re-emerges in the aligned representation, where the same
operation is represented by an invariant swap plus compensating rotations.

**(M2) Little-group phase.** For a pure rotation $R$ acting on a helicity
state,
$$
U(R)\,|p,\lambda\rangle
= e^{-i\lambda\,\phi_W(R,\hat p)}\;|Rp,\lambda\rangle .
$$ {#eq-app-littlegroup}
*Derivation.* $U(R)|p,\lambda\rangle
= U[R\,R_h(\hat p)]\,U[L_z]|s,\lambda\rangle
= U[R_h(\widehat{Rp})]\,U[W]\,U[L_z]|s,\lambda\rangle$ with the little-group
element
$$
W = R_h(\widehat{Rp})^{-1}\,R\,R_h(\hat p).
$$
$W$ maps $\hat z\to\hat p\to\widehat{Rp}\to\hat z$, so $W=R_z(\phi_W)$; it
commutes with the $z$-boost and acts on the rest state by
$e^{-i\lambda\phi_W}$. $\square$

The crucial qualitative fact: **helicity does not mix under rotations** —
rotations contribute phases only; mixing of helicity labels comes exclusively
from boosts (which is why per-chain helicity conventions generate genuine
$d^{j}(\zeta)$ crossing rotations, while a common convention leaves pure
phases).

**(M3) Rotational invariance of the dynamics**: $[\,T,\,U(R)\,]=0$.

**(M4) Kinematics of the standard configuration.** For
$\sigma_1=(p_2+p_3)^2$, $\sigma_3=(p_1+p_2)^2$ the standard configuration
$x^*(\sigma_1,\sigma_3)$ has, component-wise,
$$
E_i=\frac{M_0^2+m_i^2-\sigma_i}{2M_0}\;(i=1,3),\quad E_2=M_0-E_1-E_3,
$$
$$
p_2^*=(E_2,0,0,|\vec p_2|),\qquad
p_1^*=(E_1,\,|\vec p_1|s_{\theta},\,0,\,|\vec p_1|c_{\theta}),\qquad
p_3^* = (E_3,\,-p_{1x}^*,\,0,\,-p_{1z}^*-|\vec p_2|),
$$
with $c_\theta = \big(E_1E_2-\tfrac12(\sigma_3-m_1^2-m_2^2)\big)/
(|\vec p_1||\vec p_2|)$ and $s_\theta>0$.

## Derived, level 1: rotation covariance {#sec-app-covariance}

From (M2) applied to the proton (the kaons, $\lambda=0$, contribute no
phase), (M3), and the conjugate of the rest-state rotation rule:
$$
M_{\lambda_0\lambda_2}(R\,x)
= e^{-i\phi_W(R,\hat p_2)\,\lambda_2}
\sum_{\nu} D^{J_0*}_{\lambda_0\nu}(R)\; M_{\nu\lambda_2}(x).
$$ {#eq-app-covariance}
*Derivation.* $|f(Rx;\lambda_2)\rangle = e^{+i\phi_W\lambda_2}U(R)|f(x;\lambda_2)\rangle$
by @eq-app-littlegroup, so
$\langle f(Rx)| = e^{-i\phi_W\lambda_2}\langle f(x)|U(R)^\dagger$; commuting
$U^\dagger$ through $T$ and using
$U^\dagger|0,\lambda_0\rangle = \sum_\nu D^{J_0*}_{\lambda_0\nu}(R)\,|0,\nu\rangle$
gives the result. $\square$ (Test E; the numerics also fixes the *sign* of
the $\lambda_2$ phase — the opposite sign fails at $O(10)$.)

Defining the orientation map $R(x)$ by $x = R(x)\,x^*(\sigma_1,\sigma_3)$
(unique for generic, non-degenerate configurations) and the aligned
amplitude $A_{\nu\lambda_2}(\sigma_1,\sigma_3) \equiv
M_{\nu\lambda_2}\big(x^*(\sigma_1,\sigma_3)\big)$, @eq-app-covariance becomes
the orientation decomposition
$$
M_{\lambda_0\lambda_2}(x)
= e^{-i\phi_W\lambda_2}
\sum_{\nu} D^{J_0*}_{\lambda_0\nu}\big(R(x)\big)\;
A_{\nu\lambda_2}\big(\sigma_1(x),\sigma_3(x)\big).
$$ {#eq-app-decomposition}

## Derived, level 2: geometry of the swap {#sec-app-geometry}

Three component-wise facts, all pure kinematics:

1. **Invariants.** $\sigma_1(\tilde x)=\sigma_3(x)$,
   $\sigma_3(\tilde x)=\sigma_1(x)$ for $\tilde x = (p_3,p_2,p_1)$.
2. **Swap of the standard configuration.** Using (M4) and
   $R_z(\pi)(p_x,p_y,p_z)=(-p_x,-p_y,p_z)$, one checks slot by slot that
   $$
   P_{13}\,x^*(\sigma_1,\sigma_3) = R_z(\pi)\,x^*(\sigma_3,\sigma_1):
   $$ {#eq-app-swapgeom}
   the swapped kaon has the energy and polar angle of kaon-1 at swapped
   invariants but sits at $p_x<0$; the $\pi$-rotation about the proton axis
   restores the standard half-plane while leaving $p_2^*$ untouched.
   (Test D.)
3. **Composition of the orientation and the little-group phase.** Slot
   relabeling acts on tuple positions, rotations act on each entry, so they
   commute: $P_{13}(Rx) = R\,(P_{13}x)$. Hence
   $$
   \tilde x = R(x)\,P_{13}\,x^*(\sigma_1,\sigma_3)
   = R(x)\,R_z(\pi)\;x^*(\sigma_3,\sigma_1)
   \;\Longrightarrow\;
   R(\tilde x) = R(x)\,R_z(\pi),
   $$ {#eq-app-orientshift}
   i.e. $(\alpha,\beta,\gamma)\to(\alpha,\beta,\gamma+\pi)$. And since
   $R_z(\pi)$ fixes $\hat z$ ($=\hat p_2$ of the standard configuration),
   the little-group element composes,
   $W\big(R\,R_z(\pi)\big) = W(R)\,R_z(\pi)$:
   $$
   \phi_W(\tilde x) = \phi_W(x) + \pi .
   $$ {#eq-app-phiwshift}
   (Test F, items ii–iii, extracted from the momenta alone.)

## Derived, level 3: the cross-chain relation and the master formula {#sec-app-crosschain}

Let $T_1$ be a chain amplitude (fixed-frame function) and define the mirror
chain as its slot-swap image, $T_2 \equiv T_1\circ P_{13}$ — the "same
coupling" prescription. Evaluate $T_2$ at the standard configuration and
apply @eq-app-swapgeom, then @eq-app-covariance with $R = R_z(\pi)$, for
which $D^{J_0*}_{\lambda_0\nu}(R_z(\pi)) = e^{+i\pi\lambda_0}\,
\delta_{\lambda_0\nu}$ (diagonal!) and $\phi_W = \pi$:
$$
T_{2}\big(x^*(\sigma_1,\sigma_3)\big)
= T_1\big(R_z(\pi)\,x^*(\sigma_3,\sigma_1)\big)
= e^{+i\pi\lambda_0}\,e^{-i\pi\lambda_2}\;
T_1\big(x^*(\sigma_3,\sigma_1)\big)
= (-1)^{\lambda_0-\lambda_2}\,
T_1\big(x^*(\sigma_3,\sigma_1)\big).
$$ {#eq-app-crosschain}
(Test B.) So the cross-chain phase is *not* an independent input: it is
covariance (level 1) plus geometry (level 2). Summing the two chains gives
the master formula of the case study,
$$
A_{\lambda_0\lambda_2}(\sigma_1,\sigma_3)
= O^c_{\lambda_0\lambda_2}(\sigma_1,\sigma_3)
+ (-1)^{\lambda_0-\lambda_2}\,
O^c_{\lambda_0\lambda_2}(\sigma_3,\sigma_1),
$$
and with it the invariant-level exchange property
$A(\sigma_3,\sigma_1) = (-1)^{\lambda_0-\lambda_2} A(\sigma_1,\sigma_3)$
(test C). The diagonality of the phase is special to the spectator-2
standard orientation: only there is the compensating rotation about the axis
that simultaneously diagonalizes the parent $D$-matrix and reduces the
proton factor to a helicity eigenphase.

## Derived, level 4: closure — the theorem re-emerges {#sec-app-closure}

Insert the level-2 shifts into the decomposition @eq-app-decomposition
evaluated at $\tilde x$:

| factor | origin | value under $p_1\leftrightarrow p_3$ |
|--------|--------|--------------------------------------|
| bracket arguments | invariants swap + @eq-app-crosschain | $(-1)^{\lambda_0'-\lambda_2}$ |
| $D^{J_0*}_{\lambda_0\lambda_0'}(\alpha,\beta,\gamma+\pi)$ | orientation shift @eq-app-orientshift | $e^{+i\pi\lambda_0'}$ |
| $e^{-i\phi_W(\tilde x)\lambda_2}$ | little-group shift @eq-app-phiwshift | $e^{-i\pi\lambda_2}$ |

The last two combine to $e^{i\pi(\lambda_0'-\lambda_2)} =
(-1)^{\lambda_0'-\lambda_2}$ (an exact identity, the exponent being an
integer), so
$$
M_{\lambda_0\lambda_2}(\tilde x)
= \big[(-1)^{\lambda_0'-\lambda_2}\big]^{2}\,
M_{\lambda_0\lambda_2}(x)
= +\,M_{\lambda_0\lambda_2}(x),
$$ {#eq-app-closure}
recovering @eq-app-theorem — the statistics input (M1) — from the aligned
representation. (Test F, reconstruction.) The symmetrization phase appears
**twice**, once from the geometry and once from the bracket, and squares to
one; neither factor is meaningful in isolation.

Two corollaries:

- **The closure derives the phase.** Given the transformation laws
  @eq-app-orientshift and @eq-app-phiwshift in the $+\hat z$ spectator
  convention, *demanding* @eq-app-theorem forces the bracket phase
  $(-1)^{\lambda_0'-\lambda_2}$. Inserting
  $(-1)^{\lambda_0'+\lambda_2}$ without changing the aligned-frame convention
  yields $M(\tilde x) = -M(x)$ — the fermionic sign for the kaons. Thus the
  exponent is falsifiable only after the orientation and helicity conventions
  are specified.
- **Branch insensitivity.** For half-integer spins the SU(2) representative
  of $R(x)$ is double-valued; both $\phi_W$ (mod $4\pi$) and $D^{J_0*}$ flip
  sign together under a change of representative, so
  @eq-app-decomposition and the closure are branch-independent as long as
  the two are extracted from the *same* representative.

## Map to the published convention

The derivation above is for the $+\hat z$ spectator convention used by the toy
check. In the proton-along-$-\hat z$ convention used in @LHCb:2021XiBpKK, the
aligned standard configuration is obtained by a fixed $R_x(\pi)$ rotation.
Test G verifies directly that the cross-chain ratio then becomes
$$
\varepsilon_{\lambda_0\lambda_2}=(-1)^{\lambda_0+\lambda_2},
$$
the pattern shown in Eq. (6) of that analysis. Operationally, reversing the
aligned proton direction changes the sign of the proton little-group
contribution in the closure; Bose symmetry is unchanged.

## Generalization

The derivation used the specific spin content only in the last step. For a
parent of spin $J_0$ and a spectator of spin $s_2$ with two identical
spin-$s$ particles in slots 1, 3, the same chain gives:
statistics sign $\eta=(-1)^{2s}$ from (M1); shifts
$(\gamma,\phi_W)\to(\gamma+\pi,\phi_W+\pi)$ from level 2 in the $+\hat z$
spectator convention; bracket phase $\varepsilon_{\lambda_0'\lambda_2} =
\eta\,e^{i\pi(\lambda_0'-\lambda_2)}$ forced by closure. Reversing the
spectator quantization axis changes the sign of the spectator-helicity term,
as in the $\Xi_b^-\to pK^-K^-$ map above. Identical particles
*with* spin additionally carry their own little-group phases under the
compensating rotation. For standard orientations **not** symmetric under the
swap (e.g. chain-1 alignment), $r_\pi$ is no longer about the quantization
axis: $D^{J_0}(r_\pi)$ becomes non-diagonal and the neat phase is replaced by
genuine helicity mixing — the situation that four-body systems
([case 5](cases/05-chic2KKpipi/notes.qmd)) present generically.

## Relation–test map {#sec-app-testmap}

All in `cases/02-xib2pKK/toy_alignment_check.jl` (model: manifestly
Bose-symmetric covariant amplitude with explicit helicity spinors —
independent of any chain machinery).

| Relation | Equation | Test | Precision |
|----------|----------|------|-----------|
| Theorem from statistics | @eq-app-theorem | A | $<10^{-15}$ |
| Cross-chain phase | @eq-app-crosschain | B | exact pattern |
| Invariant-level exchange | (level 3) | C | exact pattern |
| Swap geometry | @eq-app-swapgeom | D | $<10^{-15}$ |
| Rotation covariance, sign of $\lambda_2$ phase | @eq-app-covariance | E | $<10^{-14}$ / wrong sign $O(10)$ |
| Orientation & $\phi_W$ shifts, closure | @eq-app-orientshift, @eq-app-phiwshift, @eq-app-closure | F | $<10^{-14}$ |
| Map to published $p\parallel-\hat z$ convention | fixed $R_x(\pi)$ rotation of the aligned standard configuration | G | exact sign pattern $(-1)^{\lambda_0+\lambda_2}$ |
